finding: B. SECONDARY CHONDROSARCOMA = complication of a preexisting skeletal abnormality such as

differentials.name: hyperglycemia as paraneoplastic syndrome
differentials.raw: hyperglycemia as paraneoplastic syndrome (85%) Location:   neck of femur, pubic rami, proximal humerus, ribs (19%), skull (sphenoid bone, cerebellopontine angle, mandible), sternum, spine (3-12%)

differentials.name: gradually increasing pain, often worse at night
differentials.raw: gradually increasing pain, often worse at night

differentials.name: local swelling / palpable mass (45%) Location:   pelvis, hip, scapula, sternum, ribs, ends of humerus / femur, craniofacial bones
differentials.raw: local swelling / palpable mass (45%) Location:   pelvis, hip, scapula, sternum, ribs, ends of humerus / femur, craniofacial bones

differentials.name: Osteochondroma
differentials.raw: Osteochondroma (densely calcified with multiple punctate calcifications)

differentials.name: Parosteal osteosarcoma
differentials.raw: Parosteal osteosarcoma (more homogeneous density of calcified osteoid)

differentials.name: slowly growing soft-tissue mass; pain + tenderness (33%) ◊  Metastatic in 40-45% at time of presentation!
differentials.raw: slowly growing soft-tissue mass; pain + tenderness (33%) ◊  Metastatic in 40-45% at time of presentation!

differentials.name: frequently metastasized to lungs + lymph nodes
differentials.raw: frequently metastasized to lungs + lymph nodes

differentials.name: pain + muscle swelling / contracture in presence of a mass
differentials.raw: pain + muscle swelling / contracture in presence of a mass

context: Spread:   via marrow cavity / periosteum Metastases (uncommon) to:   lung, epidural space May be associated with:  Ollier disease, Maffucci syndrome, Paget disease CT: √  chondroid matrix mineralization of "rings and arcs" (CHARACTERISTIC) in 70% √  nonmineralized portion of tumor hypodense to muscle (high water content of hyaline cartilage) √  extension into soft-tissues MR: √  low to intermediate signal intensity on T1WI √  high SI on T2WI + hypointense areas ← mineralization / fibrous septa √  enhancement of fibrous septations NUC: √  bone scan uptake intensity compared to anterior iliac cr
